provenance.source_corpus: Techniques for restoring fen vegetation on cut‐away peatlands in North America

source_spans.text: Sphagnum transfer, a reintroduction technique commonly used for bog restoration in North America, was effective for establishing Sphagnum and Carex species.
the covers of Sphagnum, Carex and total vegetation were higher on these experimental units
the covers of Sphagnum, Carex and total vegetation were higher on these experimental units
the covers of Sphagnum, Carex and total vegetation were higher on these experimental units
The treatments which included light phosphorus fertilization, had a higher Carex cover after three growing seasons.
species richness was significantly higher where vegetation had been reintroduced
species richness was significantly higher where vegetation had been reintroduced
the wet 2004 season was a good premise for the successful establishment of the Sphagnum species
the wet 2004 season was a good premise for the successful establishment of the Sphagnum species
The highest Sphagnum cover (31% ± 14) was observed for block B where the water level was just below the surface
prolonged flooding had a disastrous effect on the establishment of these species
The highest Sphagnum covers were observed for units where the mean soil water potential was just below 0
physical disturbances in the field, such as erosion and sedimentation, often have a negative effect on regeneration
physical disturbances in the field, such as erosion and sedimentation, often have a negative effect on regeneration
The timing of the reintroductions had no impact on the success of vegetation establishment
The timing of the reintroductions had no impact on the success of vegetation establishment
The exclusion of bryophytes from European restoration projects is probably due to a lack of donor vegetation sources and unsuitable water chemistry caused by eutrophication
The exclusion of bryophytes from European restoration projects is probably due to a lack of donor vegetation sources and unsuitable water chemistry caused by eutrophication
